edges.description: Failed recycling shifts CTLA4 toward degradative trafficking.
Accelerated turnover lowers steady-state CTLA4 abundance.
edges.target: CTLA4 depletion in regulatory T cells

edges.description: Reduced CTLA4 availability weakens inhibitory checkpoint signaling.
edges.source: CTLA4 depletion in regulatory T cells

edges.description: Fewer regulatory T cells reduce the capacity for checkpoint-mediated suppression.
Loss of CTLA4-mediated checkpoint suppression permits inappropriate immune activation.
Defective checkpoint control explains autoimmune cytopenias including hemolytic anemia.
intermediate_mechanisms: Autoantibody-mediated erythrocyte destruction downstream of immune dysregulation.

edges.description: Defective checkpoint control explains immune thrombocytopenia in LRBA deficiency.
intermediate_mechanisms: Immune thrombocytopenia downstream of systemic immune dysregulation.

edges.description: Immune dysregulation explains the chronic enteropathy and diarrhea phenotype.
intermediate_mechanisms: Immune-mediated enteropathy from uncontrolled lymphocyte activation.

edges.description: Loss of immune checkpoint restraint contributes to lymphoproliferative splenic enlargement.
intermediate_mechanisms: Lymphoproliferation and organomegaly from impaired immune regulation.

edges.description: Defective activation limits differentiation into memory and antibody-secreting states.
edges.target: Reduced switched memory B-cell and plasmablast compartments

edges.description: Loss of mature antibody-secreting compartments limits immunoglobulin output.
edges.source: Reduced switched memory B-cell and plasmablast compartments

edges.description: Defective immunoglobulin secretion lowers circulating immunoglobulin concentrations.
Impaired humoral immunity increases susceptibility to recurrent infections.
intermediate_mechanisms: Antibody deficiency and defective specific antibody responses.

nodes.description: CTLA4-Ig replacement therapy that addresses the checkpoint defect caused by LRBA loss. It is particularly useful for immune dysregulation, lymphoproliferation, and chronic enteropathy.

evidence.reference_title: AUTOIMMUNE DISEASE. Patients with LRBA deficiency show CTLA4 loss and immune dysregulation responsive to abatacept therapy.
evidence.snippet: We found that patients with LRBA deficiency manifested a dramatic and sustained improvement in response to abatacept, a CTLA4 (cytotoxic T lymphocyte antigen-4)-immunoglobulin fusion drug.

evidence.reference_title: Abatacept as a Long-Term Targeted Therapy for LRBA Deficiency.
evidence.snippet: Long-term abatacept therapy is effective in most patients with LRBA deficiency.

nodes.description: Allogeneic HSCT can provide durable immune reconstitution and remission in selected patients with severe or refractory disease, although transplant risk remains substantial.

evidence.reference_title: Therapeutic modalities and clinical outcomes in a large cohort with LRBA deficiency and CTLA4 insufficiency.
evidence.snippet: HSCT was performed in 14 LRBA-/- patients; 9 patients (64.2%) showed complete remission, and 3 (21.3%) continued to receive immunosuppressants after transplantation.

evidence.reference_title: Abatacept as a Long-Term Targeted Therapy for LRBA Deficiency.
evidence.snippet: Autoimmune hemolytic anemia (AIHA) and immune thrombocytopenia (ITP) were the most common hematological manifestation and observed in 9 (40.9%) and 11 (50%) patients, respectively.

evidence.reference_title: Deleterious mutations in LRBA are associated with a syndrome of immune deficiency and autoimmunity.
evidence.snippet: We conclude that mutations in LRBA cause an immune deficiency characterized by defects in B cell activation and autophagy and by susceptibility to apoptosis, all of which are associated with a clinical phenotype of hypogammaglobulinemia and autoimmunity.

nodes.description: Accelerated CTLA4 turnover in LRBA deficiency reduces CTLA4 protein abundance in FoxP3-positive regulatory T cells.
nodes.id: CTLA4 depletion in regulatory T cells

evidence.reference_title: AUTOIMMUNE DISEASE. Patients with LRBA deficiency show CTLA4 loss and immune dysregulation responsive to abatacept therapy.
evidence.snippet: We found that LRBA colocalized with CTLA4 in endosomal vesicles and that LRBA deficiency or knockdown increased CTLA4 turnover, which resulted in reduced levels of CTLA4 protein in FoxP3(+) regulatory and activated conventional T cells.

evidence.reference_title: Abatacept as a Long-Term Targeted Therapy for LRBA Deficiency.
evidence.snippet: Most patient had chronic diarrhea (CD) (n:18, 81.8%) and enteropathy was proven by biopsy in 14 (63.6%) patients.

nodes.description: LRBA-deficient plasmablasts have impaired immunoglobulin secretion and poor specific antibody responses.

evidence.reference_title: Deleterious mutations in LRBA are associated with a syndrome of immune deficiency and autoimmunity.
evidence.snippet: Individuals with homozygous LRBA mutations had no LRBA, had disturbed B cell development, defective in vitro B cell activation, plasmablast formation, and immunoglobulin secretion, and had low proliferative responses.

evidence.reference_title: The extended phenotype of LPS-responsive beige-like anchor protein (LRBA) deficiency.
evidence.snippet: Most LRBA-deficient patients had low B-cell subset counts, mainly in switched memory B cells (80%) and plasmablasts (92%), with a defective specific antibody response in 67%.

evidence.reference_title: The extended phenotype of LPS-responsive beige-like anchor protein (LRBA) deficiency.
evidence.snippet: Immune dysregulation (95%), organomegaly (86%), recurrent infections (71%), and hypogammaglobulinemia (57%) were the main clinical complications observed in LRBA-deficient patients.

nodes.description: Supportive IVIG or SCIG replacement is commonly used to manage the humoral immune deficiency and recurrent infections associated with LRBA deficiency.

evidence.reference_title: The extended phenotype of LPS-responsive beige-like anchor protein (LRBA) deficiency.
evidence.snippet: Of the 22 patients, 3 are deceased, 2 were treated successfully with hematopoietic stem cell transplantation, 7 are receiving immunoglobulin replacement, and 15 are receiving immunosuppressive treatment with systemic corticosteroids alone or in combination with steroid-sparing agents.

nodes.description: LRBA-deficient B cells show a cell-intrinsic activation defect during early effector responses.

evidence.reference_title: Deleterious mutations in LRBA are associated with a syndrome of immune deficiency and autoimmunity.
evidence.snippet: Individuals with homozygous LRBA mutations had no LRBA, had disturbed B cell development, defective in vitro B cell activation, plasmablast formation, and immunoglobulin secretion, and had low proliferative responses.

nodes.description: LRBA normally colocalizes with CTLA4 in endosomal vesicles and promotes CTLA4 recycling. Loss of LRBA disrupts this intracellular recycling step in regulatory T cells.

evidence.reference_title: AUTOIMMUNE DISEASE. Patients with LRBA deficiency show CTLA4 loss and immune dysregulation responsive to abatacept therapy.
evidence.snippet: We found that LRBA colocalized with CTLA4 in endosomal vesicles and that LRBA deficiency or knockdown increased CTLA4 turnover, which resulted in reduced levels of CTLA4 protein in FoxP3(+) regulatory and activated conventional T cells.

nodes.description: Reduced CTLA4-mediated inhibitory signaling weakens checkpoint suppression of immune activation in LRBA deficiency.

evidence.reference_title: AUTOIMMUNE DISEASE. Patients with LRBA deficiency show CTLA4 loss and immune dysregulation responsive to abatacept therapy.
evidence.snippet: We found that LRBA colocalized with CTLA4 in endosomal vesicles and that LRBA deficiency or knockdown increased CTLA4 turnover, which resulted in reduced levels of CTLA4 protein in FoxP3(+) regulatory and activated conventional T cells.

nodes.description: LRBA deficiency increases CTLA4 turnover and routing toward lysosomal degradation, accelerating loss of CTLA4 protein.

evidence.reference_title: AUTOIMMUNE DISEASE. Patients with LRBA deficiency show CTLA4 loss and immune dysregulation responsive to abatacept therapy.
evidence.snippet: We found that LRBA colocalized with CTLA4 in endosomal vesicles and that LRBA deficiency or knockdown increased CTLA4 turnover, which resulted in reduced levels of CTLA4 protein in FoxP3(+) regulatory and activated conventional T cells.

evidence.reference_title: The extended phenotype of LPS-responsive beige-like anchor protein (LRBA) deficiency.
evidence.snippet: Immune dysregulation (95%), organomegaly (86%), recurrent infections (71%), and hypogammaglobulinemia (57%) were the main clinical complications observed in LRBA-deficient patients.

nodes.description: Many patients with LRBA deficiency have reduced regulatory T-cell counts, further limiting suppressive immune control.

evidence.reference_title: The extended phenotype of LPS-responsive beige-like anchor protein (LRBA) deficiency.
evidence.snippet: Although 81% of LRBA-deficient patients had normal T-cell counts, 73% had reduced regulatory T (Treg) cell numbers.

nodes.description: LRBA deficiency is associated with depletion of switched memory B cells and plasmablasts, indicating defective B-cell maturation and effector differentiation.
nodes.id: Reduced switched memory B-cell and plasmablast compartments

evidence.reference_title: The extended phenotype of LPS-responsive beige-like anchor protein (LRBA) deficiency.
evidence.snippet: Most LRBA-deficient patients had low B-cell subset counts, mainly in switched memory B cells (80%) and plasmablasts (92%), with a defective specific antibody response in 67%.

evidence.reference_title: Abatacept as a Long-Term Targeted Therapy for LRBA Deficiency.
evidence.snippet: The third common manifestation in LRBA-deficient patients was LP, which was characterized by splenomegaly (n:14, 63.6%), hepatomegaly (n:12, 54,5%) and lymphadenopathy (n: 11, 50%).

evidence.reference_title: Abatacept as a Long-Term Targeted Therapy for LRBA Deficiency.
evidence.snippet: Autoimmune hemolytic anemia (AIHA) and immune thrombocytopenia (ITP) were the most common hematological manifestation and observed in 9 (40.9%) and 11 (50%) patients, respectively.
